Params: {1: '1', 2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'To think you\'d find your way to me... You\'re as sharp as ever, Gray Raven.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Closing the book, Ishmael flicks her fingers.'}
{3: 'A strange field warps the space around them as a gateway gently seals itself, keeping its secrets hidden from prying eyes.'}
{3: 'A faint smile plays on her lips, but she remains silent.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'Ishmael pauses, a flicker of confusion crossing her face.'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'But that\'s fine. Whatever floats your boat.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Rising from her seat, Ishmael retrieves a porcelain cup brimming with sugar cubes from beneath the table.'}
{3: 'She plucks two sugar cubes from the cup and drops them into her coffee, stirring thoroughly before pulling the cup toward you.'}
{3: 'With that faint smile, the pink-haired woman draws the coffee cup closer.'}
{3: 'Ishmael remarks, a hidden meaning in her tone.'}
{2: 'All of that is behind us now.', 3: '49'}
{2: 'So you were the one who helped me, weren\'t you?', 3: '50'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'As I\'ve told you before, I\'m just the gatekeeper; I don\'t make the rules.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Every turning point hinges on you, Gray Raven. I can only help you cover your tracks, but I can\'t dictate the "story" that unfolds.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Today, this world—all these shifts are because of you, Gray Raven.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Do you ever regret it? Carrying this burden all by yourself?', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Countless memories flood her mind—some steeped in despair and suffering, others filled with clamor or silence.'}
{3: 'Perhaps it was bitter enough to make her want to gnaw on fate\'s backbone and roar at the world\'s injustice, but... no matter what, that is already in the past.'}
{3: 'Ishmael smiles gently and shakes her head.'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'If you do have regrets, you wouldn\'t be standing here right now.', 4: '1'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Now that you\'ve found your way to me, what\'s on your mind?', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Go ahead, ask away. Maybe I\'ll even give you the straight answer.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'She smiles, her gaze dropping back to the book in her hand.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Ah... that\'s right here in this one.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Ishmael pulls a thin book from under the table and quickly thumbs through it.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Nanami... she tried to link up with Gestalt to crunch some numbers, but the word came back: Even Gestalt couldn\'t compute it.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Well, of course. The cosmic forces of causality are way beyond what the current Gestalt can handle.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'While you all were going through your transformations, she swapped out her frame and has been searching on the ground for...', 4: '1'}
{3: 'The girl, in her familiar frame, searches the ground for "the future she desires most."'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'She\'s one of a kind. Her sealed memories will unlock little by little, and she\'ll gradually piece together everything from her past.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Maybe she\'d be better off not remembering.', 3: '119'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'No one can make this choice for another.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'She offers a faint smile and closes the book.'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'I\'m sorry, but I can\'t quite spill the beans on the full scoop with the Hetero Tower.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Vonnegut said the Hetero Tower would inevitably come back...', 3: '125'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Yes, the Hetero Tower will absolutely come back.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'But, thanks to all your hard work, it might show up a little later. After all, that\'s your prize for everything you\'ve put on the line, isn\'t it?', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'With the Hetero Tower out of the picture for now, maybe Earth can finally strike a better balance of power.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Perhaps, with things this way, more futures and possibilities can truly blossom.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'The Observer would certainly be thrilled to see a world like that.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Her silence hangs in the air, a clear sign that the human still has time for questions.'}
{2: 'So, is wiping out the Punishing Virus the only way to make it through this catastrophe?', 3: '135'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'No one can answer your question... not even me.', 4: '1'}

{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Dominik and Nemo\'s timeline got wiped out by Chaos contamination, while the Four-Winged White Raven\'s civilization rotted from the inside out, consumed by the greed of Substance-0.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Ishmael turns a page of the book, and the paper crumbles to sand in her hands, trickling down with a soft rustling sound.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'No one knows how to fix this mess. This question has no answer, yet countless answers all at once.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Just as you\'ve carved a path I never saw coming, maybe humanity can cook up a totally different, truly viable solution.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'I\'m looking forward to your solution... and to the day when you and your civilization can truly walk alongside me as equals.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'The pages of the book slowly close as Ishmael turns to face the human across from her with a smile.'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Now, it\'s your turn to answer my question.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Since you managed to find your way here, you must have... gotten all your memories back, haven\'t you?', 4: '1'}
{3: 'The memories that were once a jumbled mess have all been untangled. Following a crimson thread, You\'ve read through every bit of your past.'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Making a choice like that all on your own, after thirty years...', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'Whether it\'s diving back into the ocean depths or converging the timeline, the slightest misstep would reduce everything to ashes.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'A flicker of genuine curiosity sparks in the pink-hued woman\'s eyes.'}
{2: 'No matter what choice I make, they\'ll always have my back.', 3: '166'}
{2: 'For you, maybe this is just a story.', 3: '279'}
{3: 'It\'s also the world where they are.'}
{3: 'A fleeting moment of melancholy crosses her face, only to be masked away instantly. Ishmael lets out a soft, noncommittal sigh.'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'I see... Well then, let\'s keep going.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Ishmael', 3: 'To see what new fruits your world will bear.', 4: '1'}
